hypothesis: Two-electron atom: LDA overestimates binding, HF underestimates. Exact KS potential can be obtained via reverse engineering.
method: iDEA exact solve + LDA + HF + KS potential inversion for 2e atom
expected_result: E_exact between E_LDA and E_HF; v_xc recoverable
success_criteria: All three methods converge; v_xc physically reasonable
actual_result: E_exact=-1.7076, E_LDA=-1.7476 (-2.3%), E_HF=-1.6965 (+0.7%). KS inversion converged at tol=1e-6.
lessons_learned: LDA KS gap != exact KS gap. iDEA reverse engineering slow for multi-well systems.

hypothesis: Prime-spaced quantum wells exhibit anomalous negative derivative discontinuity
method: 4-well system with prime/uniform/Fibonacci/random spacings, exact many-body solution
expected_result: Prime lattice DD significantly different from others
success_criteria: DD sign difference between prime and uniform lattices
actual_result: DD(prime) = -0.070, DD(uniform) = +0.325. But control experiment showed anti-prime {6,6,3} gives identical DD to prime-like {3,6,6}.
outcome: refuted — negative DD from spacing asymmetry, not prime structure
errors_found: Unequal lattice spans across comparison groups
lessons_learned: Always use equal-span controls. Correlation != causation.

hypothesis: Exact KS gap (from potential inversion) confirms negative DD in multi-well systems
method: Reverse-engineer exact KS potential for 3-well system at d=1.0, 1.5, 2.0, 3.0
expected_result: DD sign consistent between non-interacting gap and exact KS gap
success_criteria: Same DD sign for both methods
actual_result: d=1.0: DD flipped from -0.041 (non-interacting) to +0.084 (exact KS). All other points remained positive.
outcome: refuted — 'negative DD' was artifact of using non-interacting gap instead of exact KS gap
errors_found: Used non-interacting KS gap instead of exact KS gap in DD definition
lessons_learned: DD definition requires exact KS potential. Approximate KS gaps can give qualitatively wrong DD signs.
